description: This 3D stacked bar chart displays the cation fraction (unit: at.%) of different valence states of metallic elements in the oxide films formed on Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) with varied grain sizes, prepared by different heat treatments. The tested samples include S0 (hot-rolled, no post solution treatment), S950 (solution treated at 950 °C, finest grain size ~8.7 μm), S1050 (solution treated at 1050 °C), and S1150 (solution treated at 1150 °C, coarsest grain size ~32 μm). Quantified valence states cover Al³⁺, Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺_OX (oxide phase Fe³⁺), Fe³⁺_hy (hydroxide phase Fe³⁺), Mn⁴⁺, and Mn²⁺. Key observations show Al³⁺ is the dominant cation in all oxide films; Al content gradually decreases as grain size increases (rising solution treatment temperature), while Fe and Mn contents correspondingly increase. The S950 sample exhibits the highest Al content (90.17 at.%) in its oxide film. This figure supports the conclusion that grain refinement promotes preferential Al dissolution and Al-rich oxide film formation, improving LDS corrosion resistance.
